content.text:
1. [Home](/en-us/)
2. [Azure pricing](/en-us/pricing/)
3. Azure Container Apps pricing

# Azure Container Apps pricing

- [Request a pricing quote](/en-us/contact/pricing/)
- [Try Azure for free](/en-us/free/)

1. Overview
2. Pricing table
3. Purchase options
4. Resources
5. FAQ
6. [Azure Container Apps](/en-us/products/container-apps/)
7. 
8. [Free account](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/free/)

## Build and deploy modern apps and microservices using serverless containers

 Azure Container Apps is a fully managed serverless container service that scales dynamically based on HTTP traffic or events. 

 ﻿ 

## Explore pricing options

- ### Pay as you go

Pay for compute capacity by the second, with no long-term commitments or upfront payments. Increase or decrease consumption on demand.

[Learn more](/en-us/pricing/purchase-options/pay-as-you-go/)
- ### Azure savings plan for compute

Save money across select compute services globally by committing to spend a fixed hourly amount for 1 or 3 years, unlocking lower prices until you reach your hourly commitment. Suited for dynamic workloads while accommodating for planned or unplanned changes.

[Learn more](/en-us/pricing/offers/savings-plan-compute/)

Apply filters to customize pricing options to your needs.

Prices are estimates only and are not intended as actual price quotes. Actual pricing may vary depending on the type of agreement entered with Microsoft, date of purchase, and the currency exchange rate. Prices are calculated based on US dollars and converted using London closing spot rates that are captured in the two business days prior to the last business day of the previous month end. If the two business days prior to the end of the month fall on a bank holiday in major markets, the rate setting day is generally the day immediately preceding the two business days. This rate applies to all transactions during the upcoming month. Sign in to the [Azure pricing calculator](/en-us/pricing/calculator/) to see pricing based on your current program/offer with Microsoft. Contact an [Azure sales specialist](/en-us/contact/pricing/) for more information on pricing or to request a price quote. See [frequently asked questions](/en-us/pricing/) about Azure pricing.

Region:Central USEast USEast US 2North Central USSouth Central USWest Central USWest USWest US 2West US 3UK SouthUK WestUAE CentralUAE NorthSwitzerland NorthSwitzerland WestSweden CentralSweden SouthSpain CentralQatar CentralPoland CentralNorway EastNorway WestNew Zealand NorthMexico CentralMalaysia WestKorea CentralKorea SouthJapan EastJapan WestItaly NorthIsrael CentralIndonesia CentralCentral IndiaSouth IndiaWest IndiaGermany NorthGermany West CentralFrance CentralFrance SouthNorth EuropeWest EuropeDenmark EastChile CentralCanada CentralCanada EastBrazil SouthBrazil SoutheastBelgium CentralUS Gov ArizonaUS Gov TexasUS Gov VirginiaAustria EastAustralia CentralAustralia Central 2Australia EastAustralia SoutheastEast AsiaSoutheast AsiaSouth Africa NorthSouth Africa West

Currency:United States – Dollar ($) USDAustralia – Dollar ($) AUDBrazil – Real (R$) BRLCanada – Dollar ($) CADDenmark – Krone (kr) DKKEuro Zone – Euro (€) EURIndia – Rupee (₹) INRJapan – Yen (¥) JPYKorea – Won (₩) KRWNew Zealand – Dollar ($) NZDNorway – Krone (kr) NOKRussia – Ruble (руб) RUBSweden – Krona (kr) SEKSwitzerland – Franc (chf) CHFTaiwan – Dollar (NT$) TWDUnited Kingdom – Pound (£) GBP

US government entities are eligible to purchase Azure Government services from a licensing solution provider with no upfront financial commitment, or directly through a pay-as-you-go online subscription.

[Learn more](/en-us/explore/global-infrastructure/government/)

Important—The price in R$ is merely a reference; this is an international transaction and the final price is subject to exchange rates and the inclusion of IOF taxes. An eNF will not be issued.

US government entities are eligible to purchase Azure Government services from a licensing solution provider with no upfront financial commitment, or directly through a pay-as-you-go online subscription.

[Learn more](/en-us/explore/global-infrastructure/government/)

Important—The price in R$ is merely a reference; this is an international transaction and the final price is subject to exchange rates and the inclusion of IOF taxes. An eNF will not be issued.

# Consumption plan

 Azure Container Apps consumption plan is billed based on per-second resource allocation and requests. The first 180,000 vCPU-seconds, 360,000 GiB-seconds, and 2 million requests per subscription per month are free. Beyond that, you pay for what you use on a per second basis based on the number of vCPU-s and GiB-s your applications are allocated. 

Applications scale on-demand based on requests and events. Container Apps replicas are billed for active usage when they are running. An application can be configured to scale to zero replicas when there are no requests or events to process. No usage charges apply when an application is scaled to zero.

You can optionally configure Container Apps with a minimum number of replicas to be always running in idle mode. When an application scales down to its minimum number of replicas, usage is charged at a reduced idle rate when a replica is inactive. A replica enters active mode and is charged at the active rate when it is starting up, when it is processing requests, or when its vCPU or bandwidth usage are above the active billing thresholds^1^.

For Container Apps jobs, resources consumed by each execution are billed at the active rate from its start to completion. No usage charges apply when a job is not running executions.

Resources consumed by managed Java components are billed at the active/idle rates. You may delete components that are no longer in use to stop incurring charges related to those components.

Azure Container Apps Express and Sandboxes follow the same pay-per-second pricing as Consumption Plan.

## Resource consumption

### Active usage

^1^A replica is active when vCPU usage is above 0.01 cores or when data received is above 1,000 bytes per second.

| Meter | Pay as you go Price\* | 1-year Savings Plan Price\* | 3-year Savings Plan Price\* |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| vCPU (seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
| Memory (GiB-Seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
^\*^All prices are after Free Grant (per Month) of 180,000 vCPU-seconds and 360,000 GiB-seconds.

### Serverless GPU

 Consumption-based serverless GPUs allow near real-time inferencing for custom models, SLM fine-tuning and other machine learning tasks with instant serverless scale that goes down to zero when not in use.

| Meter | Pay as you go Price\* | 1-year Savings Plan Price\* | 3-year Savings Plan Price\* |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NC T4 v3 | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
| NC A100 v4 | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
^\*^The GPU prices shown above are in addition to the active usage vCPU and RAM prices for your Container Apps.

### Idle usage

| Meter | Pay as you go Price\* | 1-year Savings Plan Price\* | 3-year Savings Plan Price\* |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| vCPU (seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
| Memory (GiB-Seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
^\*^All prices are after Free Grant (per Month) of 180,000 vCPU-seconds and 360,000 GiB-seconds.

## Requests

Container Apps are billed based on the total number of requests^2^ processed each month. The first two million requests are included free per subscription per month.

| Meter | Pay as you go price | 1 year savings plan | 3 year savings plan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Requests | $- per million | $- per million | $- per million |
^2^A request is a single HTTP request received by an app. For TCP enabled apps, a request is a single TCP connection. Request charges do not apply to jobs.^\*^All prices are after Free Grant (per Month) of 2 Million requests. 

## Dynamic sessions

Dynamic sessions in Azure Container Apps enable you to run untrusted code securely at scale.

| Meter | Pay as you go price | 1 year savings plan | 3 year savings plan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Platform-managed built-in container (Python, Node.js, Shell) | $- per session-hour | $- per session-hour | $- per session-hour |

# Dedicated plan

Azure Container Apps Dedicated plan provides a single tenancy guarantee, access to specialized hardware, and more predictable pricing. Billing for the Dedicated plan is based on the number of vCPU seconds and gibibyte (GiB) seconds allocated across instances. In addition, you are billed a base price for the Dedicated plan management.

When creating apps in the Dedicated plan you can choose from several workload profiles, each with different amounts of vCPUs and GiBs of memory. You will be billed for the total number of vCPUs and memory provisioned in this workload profile, per second that each instance is running. More than one app can run in a single workload profile depending on how many resources each app requires and the resources available in the workload profile. These workload profiles can automatically scale out to multiple instances as needed.

| Meter | Pay as you go price | 1 year savings plan | 3 year savings plan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Management (hour)\* | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
| vCPU (hour) | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
| Memory (GiB-hour) | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
| GPU (hour) | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
^\*^This meter applies to the Dedicated plan, private endpoint, and planned maintenance. Charges are additive, if multiple features are used within an environment, charges will apply to each feature individually. 

*vCPU and memory prices do not apply to GPU workload profiles.*

*Dynamic sessions in Azure Container Apps dedicated plan has no premium or additional charges outside of the Azure Container Apps charges.*

## Azure pricing and purchasing options

## Build and deploy modern apps and microservices using serverless containers

 Azure Container Apps is a fully managed serverless container service that scales dynamically based on HTTP traffic or events. 

 ﻿ 

## Explore pricing options

- ### Pay as you go

Pay for compute capacity by the second, with no long-term commitments or upfront payments. Increase or decrease consumption on demand.

[Learn more](/en-us/pricing/purchase-options/pay-as-you-go/)
- ### Azure savings plan for compute

Save money across select compute services globally by committing to spend a fixed hourly amount for 1 or 3 years, unlocking lower prices until you reach your hourly commitment. Suited for dynamic workloads while accommodating for planned or unplanned changes.

[Learn more](/en-us/pricing/offers/savings-plan-compute/)

Apply filters to customize pricing options to your needs.

Prices are estimates only and are not intended as actual price quotes. Actual pricing may vary depending on the type of agreement entered with Microsoft, date of purchase, and the currency exchange rate. Prices are calculated based on US dollars and converted using London closing spot rates that are captured in the two business days prior to the last business day of the previous month end. If the two business days prior to the end of the month fall on a bank holiday in major markets, the rate setting day is generally the day immediately preceding the two business days. This rate applies to all transactions during the upcoming month. Sign in to the [Azure pricing calculator](/en-us/pricing/calculator/) to see pricing based on your current program/offer with Microsoft. Important—The price in R$ is merely a reference; this is an international transaction and the final price is subject to exchange rates and the inclusion of IOF taxes. An eNF will not be issued.

# Consumption plan

 Azure Container Apps consumption plan is billed based on per-second resource allocation and requests. The first 180,000 vCPU-seconds, 360,000 GiB-seconds, and 2 million requests per subscription per month are free. Beyond that, you pay for what you use on a per second basis based on the number of vCPU-s and GiB-s your applications are allocated. 

Applications scale on-demand based on requests and events. Container Apps replicas are billed for active usage when they are running. An application can be configured to scale to zero replicas when there are no requests or events to process. No usage charges apply when an application is scaled to zero.

You can optionally configure Container Apps with a minimum number of replicas to be always running in idle mode. When an application scales down to its minimum number of replicas, usage is charged at a reduced idle rate when a replica is inactive. A replica enters active mode and is charged at the active rate when it is starting up, when it is processing requests, or when its vCPU or bandwidth usage are above the active billing thresholds^1^.

For Container Apps jobs, resources consumed by each execution are billed at the active rate from its start to completion. No usage charges apply when a job is not running executions.

Resources consumed by managed Java components are billed at the active/idle rates. You may delete components that are no longer in use to stop incurring charges related to those components.

Azure Container Apps Express and Sandboxes follow the same pay-per-second pricing as Consumption Plan.

## Resource consumption

### Active usage

^1^A replica is active when vCPU usage is above 0.01 cores or when data received is above 1,000 bytes per second.

| Meter | Pay as you go Price\* | 1-year Savings Plan Price\* | 3-year Savings Plan Price\* |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| vCPU (seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
| Memory (GiB-Seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
^\*^All prices are after Free Grant (per Month) of 180,000 vCPU-seconds and 360,000 GiB-seconds.

### Serverless GPU

 Consumption-based serverless GPUs allow near real-time inferencing for custom models, SLM fine-tuning and other machine learning tasks with instant serverless scale that goes down to zero when not in use.

| Meter | Pay as you go Price\* | 1-year Savings Plan Price\* | 3-year Savings Plan Price\* |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NC T4 v3 | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
| NC A100 v4 | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
^\*^The GPU prices shown above are in addition to the active usage vCPU and RAM prices for your Container Apps.

### Idle usage

| Meter | Pay as you go Price\* | 1-year Savings Plan Price\* | 3-year Savings Plan Price\* |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| vCPU (seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
| Memory (GiB-Seconds) | $- per second | $- per second | $- per second |
^\*^All prices are after Free Grant (per Month) of 180,000 vCPU-seconds and 360,000 GiB-seconds.

## Requests

Container Apps are billed based on the total number of requests^2^ processed each month. The first two million requests are included free per subscription per month.

| Meter | Pay as you go price | 1 year savings plan | 3 year savings plan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Requests | $- per million | $- per million | $- per million |
^2^A request is a single HTTP request received by an app. For TCP enabled apps, a request is a single TCP connection. Request charges do not apply to jobs.^\*^All prices are after Free Grant (per Month) of 2 Million requests. 

## Dynamic sessions

Dynamic sessions in Azure Container Apps enable you to run untrusted code securely at scale.

| Meter | Pay as you go price | 1 year savings plan | 3 year savings plan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Platform-managed built-in container (Python, Node.js, Shell) | $- per session-hour | $- per session-hour | $- per session-hour |

# Dedicated plan

Azure Container Apps Dedicated plan provides a single tenancy guarantee, access to specialized hardware, and more predictable pricing. Billing for the Dedicated plan is based on the number of vCPU seconds and gibibyte (GiB) seconds allocated across instances. In addition, you are billed a base price for the Dedicated plan management.

When creating apps in the Dedicated plan you can choose from several workload profiles, each with different amounts of vCPUs and GiBs of memory. You will be billed for the total number of vCPUs and memory provisioned in this workload profile, per second that each instance is running. More than one app can run in a single workload profile depending on how many resources each app requires and the resources available in the workload profile. These workload profiles can automatically scale out to multiple instances as needed.

| Meter | Pay as you go price | 1 year savings plan | 3 year savings plan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Management (hour)\* | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
| vCPU (hour) | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
| Memory (GiB-hour) | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
| GPU (hour) | $- per hour | $- per hour | $- per hour |
^\*^This meter applies to the Dedicated plan, private endpoint, and planned maintenance. Charges are additive, if multiple features are used within an environment, charges will apply to each feature individually. 

*vCPU and memory prices do not apply to GPU workload profiles.*

*Dynamic sessions in Azure Container Apps dedicated plan has no premium or additional charges outside of the Azure Container Apps charges.*

content.text:
I now have the full picture from the actual Terraform (app stack + harness). One caveat up front: the pricing page hides exact numbers behind JavaScript, so I'm using the standard US-region ACA consumption list rates (≈$63/month per always-active vCPU, ≈$7.90/month per always-idle vCPU, ≈$7.90/month per GiB — idle discount applies to CPU only, memory bills the same either way). Also note: **the local `az` CLI is logged in as demo user Olivia** (leftover from the go-live workout) with Reader on one resource only, so I couldn't pull your real meter data — these are computed from the deployed sizes.

## What's actually deployed (from Terraform)

**App stack — all `min_replicas ≥ 1`, so always allocated:**

| App | Size | Always-on |
|---|---|---|
| `dataq-app-worker` (Celery + beat) | 1.0 vCPU / 2 GiB | yes (beat can't scale to zero) |
| `dataq-app-api` | 0.5 / 1 GiB (max 3) | yes |
| `dataq-app-redis` | 0.5 / 1 GiB | yes |
| `dataq-app-frontend` | 0.25 / 0.5 GiB (max 2) | yes |
| migrate job | 0.5 / 1 GiB | per-run, negligible |

Total: **2.25 vCPU / 4.5 GiB allocated 24/7.**

**Harness adds — this is the surprise:**

| App | Size | Always-on |
|---|---|---|
| Airflow scheduler+webserver | 2.0 vCPU / 4 GiB | yes |
| Airflow workers | 2.0 / 4 GiB **× min 2** | yes — **4.0 vCPU / 8 GiB** |
| harness redis | 0.5 / 1 GiB | yes |
| mockdata cron jobs, ADF (2 triggers), Event Grid, ADLS | — | pennies |

Total: **+6.5 vCPU / +13 GiB always allocated — the harness is ~3× the app's compute.**

## Monthly cost estimate (pay-as-you-go, after free grants)

| Scenario | All-idle (best) | Realistic† | All-active (worst) |
|---|---|---|---|
| App only (harness off) | ~$50 | **~$100–140** | ~$175 |
| Harness only | ~$155 | **~$350–450** | ~$515 |
| **Total, harness on** | ~$205 | **~$450–560** | ~$690 |

†"Realistic" assumes the Celery worker, both redises, the Airflow scheduler and its 2 workers bill *active* (constant broker polling/heartbeats usually exceed the 1,000 bytes/s idle threshold), while api/frontend/webserver sit idle. The idle-vs-active classification is the single biggest uncertainty — only the real bill settles it.

Everything else is roughly free: **Postgres is $0** (`B_Standard_B1ms` + 32 GB is exactly the 12-month free quantity, 750 h/month), Log Analytics likely under the 5 GB free, ADF ~$2–5, KV/Event Grid/storage/bandwidth ~$1–2.

## The urgent implication

If the harness has been fully up since deploy, burn is plausibly **$15–20/day — meaning the $200 credit could run out in days, well before the 30-day mark** (services get disabled at credit exhaustion, not at day 30). I'd check the real balance today: portal → Subscriptions → your Free Trial → credit remaining. If you `az login` as the owner account, I can pull Cost Management month-to-date and replace these estimates with actuals.

## The levers, in order of impact

1. **Scale the harness to zero when not demoing** (Airflow workers min 2→0, scheduler/webserver min 1→0, harness redis off): saves ~$350–450/month — by far the biggest lever, and reversible in one `az containerapp update` / TF variable per app.
2. **Trim the app**: api min 1→0 (cold starts behind the nginx proxy are the tradeoff), worker 1.0→0.5 vCPU (keep 2 GiB for GX/pandas headroom): app drops to roughly **$50–75/month** — that's the realistic steady-state bill if you upgrade to PAYG and keep prod alive through the cycle.
3. Requests never matter here (2M/month free, health probes don't count).

So the upgrade decision from earlier now has real numbers: **harness-off + trimmed app ≈ $50–75/month** to keep prod alive past day 30; harness-on continuously is ~$500/month and isn't worth it — wake it for demo/E2E windows only. Want me to fold a "scale-to-zero harness + app trim" task into #590/#591?

content.text:
Full actuals now, from Cost Management (your billing currency is CAD). The picture is sharp:

## Real burn — CAD $21.69/day average (July 1–3), $144.80 consumed since the June 27 deploy

| Resource | CAD/day | Share |
|---|---|---|
| `dataq-harness-airflow` (scheduler+webserver, 2 vCPU/4Gi) | 8.99 | ← biggest single line |
| `dataq-harness-airflow-worker` (2× 2 vCPU/4Gi) | 6.92 | |
| `dataq-harness-redis` + `airflow-trigger` + ADF | 1.50 | |
| **Harness subtotal** | **~17.41** | **80%** |
| `dataq-app-worker` / `api` / `redis` / `frontend` | 4.04 | 19% |
| ACR (Basic, base fee) | 0.24 | |
| Postgres B1ms, Key Vault, logs, storage, mockdata jobs, alerts | **0.00** | free tiers doing their job |

So monthly: **harness ON ≈ CAD $660/mo (~USD $480)** · **harness OFF ≈ CAD $130/mo (~USD $95)** — my Terraform-based estimate held up, and the split answers your question directly: **the harness is 80% of the bill; the app itself is ~$4/day.**

## The runway problem

The Canada-localized free credit is normally **CAD $260** (the "$200" USD grant — please confirm the exact balance in portal → Subscriptions → Free Trial, the API doesn't expose it). If so:

- **Remaining ≈ CAD $115** → at $21.7/day the credit dies **~July 9–10** — not "3 weeks from now." The subscription gets disabled at credit exhaustion, not at day 30.
- **Scale the harness down today** and burn drops to ~$4.3/day → the same $115 lasts **~26 days**, comfortably past the day-30 mark (~July 26). The W1–3 plan timeline survives without paying anything.

## Recommendation

Stop the four harness apps now (`az containerapp stop` on `dataq-harness-airflow`, `-airflow-worker`, `-airflow-trigger`, `-redis` — fully reversible with `start` for demo/E2E windows). Two caveats: DataQ's 10-min Airflow polling will log connection failures while it's down (possible #419 alert noise — snooze or disable the Airflow connection's suites), and the Flow B/C harness legs are dead until restarted. ADF ($0.27/day) can stay.

This also refines the earlier upgrade math: post-upgrade PAYG steady state is **~CAD $130/mo as-deployed, likely ~CAD $60–80 trimmed** (api scale-to-zero, worker 0.5 vCPU), with the harness woken only when needed.

Want me to stop the harness apps now and fold this (credit-runway note + harness-wake runbook) into #590/#591?

BASE="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6/resourceGroups/dataq-rg/providers/Microsoft.App/containerApps"
for app in dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow-trigger dataq-harness-redis; do
  az rest --method POST --url "$BASE/$app/stop?api-version=2024-03-01" -o none 2>&1 && echo "$app: stop requested"
done

input.command:
gh issue comment 590 -R TheurgicDuke771/DataQ --body "**Decision + action record (2026-07-04): no PAYG upgrade — wrap up by the day-30 mark (~2026-07-26); harness compute STOPPED today.**

**Cost actuals that drove it** (Cost Management, billing currency CAD): burn was **CAD ~21.7/day** (~660/mo), of which the harness was **80%** — \`dataq-harness-airflow\` 8.99/day + 2× \`airflow-worker\` 6.92/day + redis/trigger/ADF ~1.50/day; the app itself is only ~4.04/day; ACR Basic 0.24/day; Postgres B1ms/32GB = \$0 (12-month free SKU). **CAD 144.80 of the free credit was already consumed by Jul 4** — at the old rate the credit would have died ~Jul 9–10, well before day 30. With the harness stopped, burn drops to ~CAD 4.5/day and the remaining credit (~115 if the grant is the standard CAD 260 — *verify balance in portal → Subscriptions → Free Trial*) comfortably reaches day 30. **W3's due date (2026-07-25) is now a hard wrap-up deadline.**

**Stopped/suspended today (all reversible):**
- \`az containerapp stop\`(ARM \`/stop\`): \`dataq-harness-airflow\`, \`dataq-harness-airflow-worker\`, \`dataq-harness-airflow-trigger\`, \`dataq-harness-redis\` — verified \`Stopped\`; the 4 \`dataq-app-*\` apps untouched and \`Running\` (frontend 200 ✓)
- ADF triggers \`tr_customers_daily\` + \`tr_orders_landed\` → \`Stopped\` (\`az datafactory trigger stop\`)
- 5 \`mockdata-*\` cron jobs → suspend accepted via \`POST .../jobs/{name}/suspend?api-version=2024-08-02-preview\` (GET doesn't surface the state on this API version — verify no new executions; cost is ~\$0 either way)

**Consequences while stopped:** Flows B/C (Airflow + ADF legs) are dead until woken; DataQ's 10-min Airflow REST poll will log connection failures (log noise only — no run-failure alerts since no runs dispatch); Flow A dies separately with the Snowflake lapse (#588).

**Wake runbook (for demo/E2E windows, incl. this issue's final live E2E):**
1. \`az containerapp start -n dataq-harness-redis -g dataq-rg\` (broker first)
2. \`az containerapp start\` for \`dataq-harness-airflow\` then \`dataq-harness-airflow-worker\` then \`-airflow-trigger\`
3. \`az datafactory trigger start\` for both triggers; resume mockdata jobs via \`POST .../jobs/{name}/resume?api-version=2024-08-02-preview\`
4. Reverse order to stop again; budget ~CAD 17/day while awake.

Also noted en route: local \`az\` had been left logged in as demo user **Olivia** since the go-live workout (now owner) — worth a mention in the teardown's credential-hygiene pass." >/dev/null && echo "590 commented"
gh issue comment 591 -R TheurgicDuke771/DataQ --body "Data point for the posture doc (2026-07-04, from the #590 wind-down): with harness compute stopped, the app-only Azure footprint costs ~CAD 4.5/day (~130/mo) as deployed — \`worker\` 1.94/day, \`api\` 0.88, \`redis\` 0.83, \`frontend\` 0.39, ACR-Basic 0.24, PG/KV/logs \$0. If a paid-Azure option is ever revisited, the trimmed steady state (api scale-to-zero, worker 0.5 vCPU) is ~CAD 60–80/mo. Decision of record: **no PAYG upgrade — local-first after day 30 (~2026-07-26)**, which makes this issue the landing surface." >/dev/null && echo "591 commented"

name: project-azure-winddown
description: Azure free-trial wind-down — harness compute STOPPED 2026-07-04, no PAYG upgrade, hard wrap-up by day-30 (~2026-07-26); cost actuals + wake runbook on #590
metadata:
  type: project
---

Decision (2026-07-04): **no pay-as-you-go upgrade** — DataQ wraps up its Azure presence by
the free-trial day-30 mark (**~2026-07-26**; W3 milestone due 2026-07-25 is the hard
deadline for #590 teardown + #591 local-first posture). Snowflake subscription lapses
separately ~2026-07-05..10 (v1.1 W1, #588).

**Why:** Cost Management actuals (billing currency **CAD**): burn was ~21.7/day
(harness = 80% — Airflow scheduler 8.99 + 2×2-vCPU workers 6.92/day); CAD 144.80 of the
free credit already consumed by Jul 4 → credit would have died ~Jul 9–10. Postgres
B1ms/32GB is $0 (12-month free SKU). App-only burn ~CAD 4.5/day.

**State since 2026-07-04:** the 4 harness container apps (`dataq-harness-airflow`,
`-airflow-worker`, `-airflow-trigger`, `-redis`) are **Stopped**; both ADF triggers
(`tr_customers_daily`, `tr_orders_landed`) **Stopped**; 5 `mockdata-*` cron jobs
**suspended** (api-version `2024-08-02-preview`). The 4 `dataq-app-*` apps still run.
Flows B/C dead until woken; DataQ's 10-min Airflow poll logs failures (noise only).
**Wake runbook is in the #590 comment** (start redis → airflow → worker → trigger;
~CAD 17/day while awake).

**How to apply:** don't treat stopped harness apps / failing Airflow polls as incidents;
before any live E2E, wake per the runbook and stop again after. `az` CLI: was left logged
in as demo user Olivia after go-live; owner login is royarijit04@outlook.com (relevant to
[[project-live-connections-and-demo]] credential hygiene at teardown).

content.content:
<system-reminder>This memory is 5 days old. Memories are point-in-time observations, not live state — claims about code behavior or file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code before asserting as fact.</system-reminder>
1	- [DataQ — Orchestration providers, not datasources](dataq_adf_is_orchestration.md) — ADF + Airflow share an OrchestrationProvider abstraction; monitor + detect failure + trigger suite on success only; never queryable datasources
2	- [DataQ — Working agreements](feedback_working_agreements.md) — single-functionality commits, manual test before next feature, GitHub issues for defects (no silent fixes), conda + Black for Python, locked coding structure
3	- [DataQ — /review before merge](feedback_review_before_merge.md) — spawn the /code-review AGENTS (never an inline self-review) on every PR; any deferred finding must be filed/noted, never silently dropped
4	- [DataQ — Verify locally before push](feedback_verify_locally_before_push.md) — run format/lint/typecheck/**bandit**/tests locally before commit; don't use CI as the first feedback loop (Ruff noqa ≠ Bandit B105)
5	- [DataQ — Fixup commits for in-review PRs](feedback_fixup_commits_in_review.md) — revise open PRs with a follow-up `fixup:` commit + plain push, never amend + force-push (squash-merge collapses it anyway)
6	- [DataQ — Test failure modes, not happy paths](feedback_test_failure_modes.md) — adversarial-input battery for data-ingesting code (backend/tests/support/adversarial.py); don't mock the seam under test; periodic mutmut spikes; ~94% line coverage still missed profiler 500s (#145/#147)
7	- [DataQ — GitHub hygiene: labels + milestones](feedback_issue_milestone.md) — every issue gets label(s) + milestone; every PR gets a milestone (feature-week; labels aren't a PR convention); closed-week milestones need the API-by-number set, not `gh pr edit`
8	- [DataQ — Local shell is Fish](dev-shell-is-fish.md) — Bash-tool commands run under Fish: quote glob flags (`--include="*.md"`), no `timeout` on macOS (use run_in_background), avoid `cd &&` chaining
9	- [DataQ — No creds in tracked files](feedback_no_creds_in_tracked_files.md) — never commit a credential (even mock/local); env templates ship blank, generate the local-dev password in setup.sh into the gitignored .env
10	- [DataQ — gh poisoned-cache 401](dev-gh-poisoned-cache-401.md) — `gh pr checks/merge` 401 "Bad credentials" while `gh api` works = stale entry in `~/.cache/gh`; rm the "Bad credentials" file, don't swap to REST
11	- [DataQ — External harness env](project_harness_env.md) — ADR 0021 harness at `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/` (not git-tracked); §1 terraform/ (plan-clean, unapplied) + §2 mockdata/ generator built; tracked by gitignored HARNESS_TODO.md
12	- [DataQ — Restart Container Apps after Postgres recreate](dev-aca-secret-restart-after-pg-recreate.md) — secret-injected env is a start-time snapshot; delete-recreate of shared PG → restart every dependent ACA revision or it resolves the old/dead host (caused the Airflow "Ooops!" 500)
13	- [DataQ — Live prod connections + demo users](project_live_connections_and_demo.md) — 5 live connections (snowflake/uc/adls/adf/airflow) with readable KV secret names; SF PAT (90d) + ADLS account SAS (exp 2027-06-28) to rotate; demo users + access on the "Retail Orders" suite; how to reach the prod DB from local

- [DataQ — /review before merge](feedback_review_before_merge.md) — spawn the /code-review AGENTS (never an inline self-review) on every PR; any deferred finding must be filed/noted, never silently dropped
- [DataQ — Verify locally before push](feedback_verify_locally_before_push.md) — run format/lint/typecheck/**bandit**/tests locally before commit; don't use CI as the first feedback loop (Ruff noqa ≠ Bandit B105)
- [DataQ — Fixup commits for in-review PRs](feedback_fixup_commits_in_review.md) — revise open PRs with a follow-up `fixup:` commit + plain push, never amend + force-push (squash-merge collapses it anyway)
- [DataQ — Test failure modes, not happy paths](feedback_test_failure_modes.md) — adversarial-input battery for data-ingesting code (backend/tests/support/adversarial.py); don't mock the seam under test; periodic mutmut spikes; ~94% line coverage still missed profiler 500s (#145/#147)

- [DataQ — Local shell is Fish](dev-shell-is-fish.md) — Bash-tool commands run under Fish: quote glob flags (`--include="*.md"`), no `timeout` on macOS (use run_in_background), avoid `cd &&` chaining

Done — the harness is stopped and the wind-down decision is locked in. Summary:

**Stopped today (all reversible, verified):**
- Container apps `dataq-harness-airflow`, `-airflow-worker`, `-airflow-trigger`, `-redis` → **Stopped**; the four `dataq-app-*` apps untouched and Running (frontend probed 200 ✓)
- ADF triggers `tr_customers_daily` + `tr_orders_landed` → **Stopped**
- The 5 `mockdata-*` cron jobs → **suspended**

**Effect on the money:** burn drops from ~CAD 21.7/day to ~**CAD 4.5/day**. With CAD 144.80 already consumed, the remaining credit (~115 if the grant is the standard CAD 260) now comfortably reaches the day-30 mark (~July 26) instead of dying next week. Worth doing two checks yourself: the exact credit balance in portal → Subscriptions → Free Trial (the API doesn't expose it), and a glance at tomorrow's cost to confirm the drop landed.

**Recorded:** the full decision + cost actuals + a **wake runbook** (redis → airflow → worker → trigger, ~CAD 17/day while awake) as a comment on [#590](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/590), the app-only cost datapoint on [#591](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/591), and a cross-session memory note so future sessions treat stopped harness apps and failing Airflow polls as expected state, not incidents.

**What this means for the plan:** the W3 milestone due date (2026-07-25) is now the hard wrap-up deadline — final live E2E (wake the harness for it, then stop it again), the #492 decision, #590 teardown, and #591 local-first posture all land by then. Two side-findings for the teardown's hygiene pass: local `az` had been sitting logged in as demo user Olivia since the go-live workout, and the ACR (`dataqacr3erlgd`, Basic) is a small standing cost the Terraform teardown should catch.

Flows B/C are dead until woken, and Flow A goes with Snowflake this week per #588 — so the W1 Snowflake-live trio is now the only clock ticking in days.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.
#
# The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
# awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
# it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.
#
# Usage:
#   harness_window.sh status           # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start            # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on, jobs resumed
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf]      # kick the 5 mockdata jobs (+ optionally the Flow-A ADF pipelines*)
#   harness_window.sh stop             # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf]   # start -> run -> stop, one shot
#
# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
set -euo pipefail

SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
RG="dataq-rg"
ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
APP_API="2024-03-01"
JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"

# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
APPS_START=(dataq-harness-redis dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow-trigger)
APPS_STOP=(dataq-harness-airflow-trigger dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-redis)
TRIGGERS=(tr_customers_daily tr_orders_landed)
JOBS=(mockdata-orders mockdata-inventory mockdata-tracking mockdata-feedback mockdata-supply)

log() { printf '%s %s\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" "$*"; }

app_state() { az containerapp show -n "$1" -g "$RG" --query properties.runningStatus -o tsv 2>/dev/null; }

wait_app() { # wait_app <name> <wanted-state>
  local name=$1 want=$2 i
  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    [[ "$(app_state "$name")" == "$want" ]] && { log "  $name -> $want"; return 0; }
    sleep 5
  done
  log "  TIMEOUT: $name never reached $want"; return 1
}

cmd_status() {
  az containerapp list -g "$RG" --query "[?starts_with(name,'dataq-harness')].{name:name, running:properties.runningStatus}" -o table
  az datafactory trigger list -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --query "[].{name:name, state:properties.runtimeState}" -o table
  local j
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    printf '%s last execution: ' "$j"
    az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null |
      python3 -c "import json,sys; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['value']; print(v[0]['properties']['status']+' @ '+v[0]['properties']['startTime'] if v else 'none')"
  done
}

cmd_start() {
  local app t j
  log "Waking harness (billing ~CAD 0.70/hour while awake)..."
  for app in "${APPS_START[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/start?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Running
    if [[ "$app" == "dataq-harness-airflow" ]]; then
      log "  waiting for Airflow /health..."
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        curl -sf --max-time 10 "$AIRFLOW_URL/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { log "  Airflow healthy"; break; }
        sleep 10
      done
    fi
  done
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger start -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Started"
  done
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/resume?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && log "  job $j resumed" || true
  done
  log "Harness is UP. Remember to '$0 stop' when done."
}

cmd_run() {
  local with_adf=${1:-} j execs=()
  log "Starting the 5 mockdata job executions (parallel)..."
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    local ename
    ename=$(az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/start?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name',''))")
    log "  $j -> execution ${ename:-<unknown>}"
    execs+=("$j:$ename")
  done
  log "Waiting for job executions to finish (timeout 35m each)..."
  local pair status
  for pair in "${execs[@]}"; do
    j=${pair%%:*}; local e=${pair#*:}
    [[ -z "$e" ]] && continue
    for _ in $(seq 1 70); do
      status=$(az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions/$e?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['properties']['status'])" 2>/dev/null || echo Unknown)
      [[ "$status" != "Running" && "$status" != "Unknown" ]] && break
      sleep 30
    done
    log "  $j: $status"
  done
  if [[ "$with_adf" == "--adf" ]]; then
    log "Triggering Flow-A ADF pipelines (needs live Snowflake!)..."
    local p rid
    for p in pl_flow_a_customers pl_flow_a_orders; do
      rid=$(az datafactory pipeline create-run -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$p" --query runId -o tsv)
      log "  $p -> run $rid"
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        status=$(az datafactory pipeline-run show -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --run-id "$rid" --query status -o tsv)
        [[ "$status" != "InProgress" && "$status" != "Queued" ]] && break
        sleep 15
      done
      log "  $p: $status"
    done
  fi
  log "Run phase done. Airflow DAGs fire on their own cron while awake (or trigger in the UI: $AIRFLOW_URL)."
}

cmd_stop() {
  local t j app
  log "Putting the harness back to sleep..."
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger stop -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Stopped"
  done
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && log "  job $j suspended" || true
  done
  for app in "${APPS_STOP[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/stop?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Stopped
  done
  log "Harness is DOWN (burn back to ~CAD 4.5/day, app only)."
}

case "${1:-}" in
  status) cmd_status ;;
  start)  cmd_start ;;
  run)    cmd_run "${2:-}" ;;
  stop)   cmd_stop ;;
  window) cmd_start; cmd_run "${2:-}"; cmd_stop ;;
  *) grep '^#' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//' | head -20; exit 1 ;;
esac

Done. The script is at **`~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/scripts/harness_window.sh`** (harness-side, deliberately not in the repo per ADR 0021). For ad-hoc testing you just call:

```bash
~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/scripts/harness_window.sh window        # start → run flows → stop, one shot
~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/scripts/harness_window.sh window --adf  # same, + Flow-A ADF pipelines (needs live Snowflake)
```

Or the pieces individually: `status` (read-only), `start`, `run [--adf]`, `stop`.

What it does:

- **`start`** wakes things in dependency order — redis → Airflow scheduler (waits for `/health` to go green) → workers → trigger app — then starts both ADF triggers and resumes the 5 mockdata cron jobs. It reminds you it's billing (~CAD 0.70/hour while awake).
- **`run`** kicks all 5 mockdata job executions in parallel and polls each to completion (they land fresh files, which the ADF blob trigger picks up while awake). `--adf` additionally `create-run`s `pl_flow_a_customers` + `pl_flow_a_orders` and polls them — gated behind a flag because Flow A loads Snowflake, which dies this week (#588). Airflow DAGs aren't force-triggered; they fire on their own cron while the scheduler is awake (the script prints the UI URL if you want to trigger one manually).
- **`stop`** reverses everything: ADF triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped in reverse order, each verified `Stopped`.

Verification status, honestly: `bash -n` clean, and `status` ran against the live subscription — it correctly shows all four apps `Stopped`, both triggers `Stopped`, and the last (pre-suspension) job executions. The stop/suspend/trigger/executions ARM calls are the exact ones proven earlier today; the only paths not yet exercised are app `/start` and the Airflow health wait, since running them would wake the harness and spend credit. First real use — likely the W3 final live E2E, or your next ad-hoc test — is the true end-to-end validation; if `start` misbehaves I'd suspect only the health-wait timing, not the calls.

Also recorded: a pointer comment on [#590](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/590) (the script supersedes the manual runbook there) and the cross-session memory note now references the script path.

content.text: the schedule jobs will run as manual with this script right?

lastPrompt: the schedule jobs will run as manual with this script right?

content.text:
Mostly yes, with one exception worth spelling out — and one edge I can verify right now for a fraction of a cent.

- **Mockdata jobs (the 5 ACA cron jobs): yes.** `run` fires them via the on-demand `start` endpoint — they execute immediately as manual executions, no waiting for their cron. The cron itself is also resumed during a window, so if a job's scheduled time happens to land inside your window it can *additionally* fire once on schedule.
- **ADF (`--adf`): yes.** `create-run` is a manual pipeline run, bypassing the daily schedule trigger (which, in a short window, would likely never fire on its own). Note the blob-event trigger is live during the window, so `pl_flow_a_orders` may also fire automatically when the mockdata files land — you can see it twice.
- **Airflow DAGs: no — the exception.** The script doesn't force-trigger DAG runs; they only fire if their cron lands inside the window (or you trigger manually in the Airflow UI). I can add a `--dags` flag that POSTs to the Airflow REST API (`/api/v1/dags/{id}/dagRuns`) if you want full one-shot coverage — it needs the Airflow credential from Key Vault.

DataQ-side: manual runs are ingested exactly like scheduled ones (the 10-min poll picks up all runs regardless of how they were triggered), so trigger bindings still fire on success.

One edge to verify: whether a manual `start` works while a job is **suspended** (matters if someone calls `run` without `start` first). Let me test that now — one mockdata job, costs ~a cent, no downstream fires since the blob trigger is stopped.

EXEC=$(az rest --method POST --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6/resourceGroups/dataq-rg/providers/Microsoft.App/jobs/mockdata-feedback/start?api-version=2024-08-02-preview" -o json 2>&1 | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name','FAILED'))" 2>/dev/null || echo FAILED)
echo "manual start while suspended -> execution: $EXEC"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.
#
# The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
# awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
# it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.
#
# Usage:
#   harness_window.sh status           # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start            # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on, jobs resumed
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf]      # kick the 5 mockdata jobs (+ optionally the Flow-A ADF pipelines*)
#   harness_window.sh stop             # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf]   # start -> run -> stop, one shot
#
# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
set -euo pipefail

SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
RG="dataq-rg"
ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
APP_API="2024-03-01"
JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"

# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
APPS_START=(dataq-harness-redis dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow-trigger)
APPS_STOP=(dataq-harness-airflow-trigger dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-redis)
TRIGGERS=(tr_customers_daily tr_orders_landed)
JOBS=(mockdata-orders mockdata-inventory mockdata-tracking mockdata-feedback mockdata-supply)

log() { printf '%s %s\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" "$*"; }

app_state() { az containerapp show -n "$1" -g "$RG" --query properties.runningStatus -o tsv 2>/dev/null; }

wait_app() { # wait_app <name> <wanted-state>
  local name=$1 want=$2 i
  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    [[ "$(app_state "$name")" == "$want" ]] && { log "  $name -> $want"; return 0; }
    sleep 5
  done
  log "  TIMEOUT: $name never reached $want"; return 1
}

cmd_status() {
  az containerapp list -g "$RG" --query "[?starts_with(name,'dataq-harness')].{name:name, running:properties.runningStatus}" -o table
  az datafactory trigger list -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --query "[].{name:name, state:properties.runtimeState}" -o table
  local j
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    printf '%s last execution: ' "$j"
    az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null |
      python3 -c "import json,sys; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['value']; print(v[0]['properties']['status']+' @ '+v[0]['properties']['startTime'] if v else 'none')"
  done
}

cmd_start() {
  local app t j
  log "Waking harness (billing ~CAD 0.70/hour while awake)..."
  for app in "${APPS_START[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/start?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Running
    if [[ "$app" == "dataq-harness-airflow" ]]; then
      log "  waiting for Airflow /health..."
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        curl -sf --max-time 10 "$AIRFLOW_URL/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { log "  Airflow healthy"; break; }
        sleep 10
      done
    fi
  done
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger start -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Started"
  done
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/resume?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && log "  job $j resumed" || true
  done
  log "Harness is UP. Remember to '$0 stop' when done."
}

cmd_run() {
  local with_adf=${1:-} j execs=()
  log "Starting the 5 mockdata job executions (parallel)..."
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    local ename
    ename=$(az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/start?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name',''))")
    log "  $j -> execution ${ename:-<unknown>}"
    execs+=("$j:$ename")
  done
  log "Waiting for job executions to finish (timeout 35m each)..."
  local pair status
  for pair in "${execs[@]}"; do
    j=${pair%%:*}; local e=${pair#*:}
    [[ -z "$e" ]] && continue
    for _ in $(seq 1 70); do
      status=$(az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions/$e?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['properties']['status'])" 2>/dev/null || echo Unknown)
      [[ "$status" != "Running" && "$status" != "Unknown" ]] && break
      sleep 30
    done
    log "  $j: $status"
  done
  if [[ "$with_adf" == "--adf" ]]; then
    log "Triggering Flow-A ADF pipelines (needs live Snowflake!)..."
    local p rid
    for p in pl_flow_a_customers pl_flow_a_orders; do
      rid=$(az datafactory pipeline create-run -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$p" --query runId -o tsv)
      log "  $p -> run $rid"
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        status=$(az datafactory pipeline-run show -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --run-id "$rid" --query status -o tsv)
        [[ "$status" != "InProgress" && "$status" != "Queued" ]] && break
        sleep 15
      done
      log "  $p: $status"
    done
  fi
  log "Run phase done. Airflow DAGs fire on their own cron while awake (or trigger in the UI: $AIRFLOW_URL)."
}

cmd_stop() {
  local t j app
  log "Putting the harness back to sleep..."
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger stop -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Stopped"
  done
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && log "  job $j suspended" || true
  done
  for app in "${APPS_STOP[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/stop?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Stopped
  done
  log "Harness is DOWN (burn back to ~CAD 4.5/day, app only)."
}

case "${1:-}" in
  status) cmd_status ;;
  start)  cmd_start ;;
  run)    cmd_run "${2:-}" ;;
  stop)   cmd_stop ;;
  window) cmd_start; cmd_run "${2:-}"; cmd_stop ;;
  *) grep '^#' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//' | head -20; exit 1 ;;
esac

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.
#
# The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
# awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
# it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.
#
# Usage:
#   harness_window.sh status           # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start            # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf]      # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
#                                      #   (+ --adf: manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*)
#   harness_window.sh stop             # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf]   # start -> run -> stop, one shot
#
# Job semantics: a suspended ACA job REFUSES manual starts, so `run` resumes each
# job, starts the execution, and suspends again at the end — everything executes
# on demand, never waiting for (or leaving armed) the cron schedules. `run` works
# standalone (mockdata jobs have no dependency on the Airflow apps).
#
# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
set -euo pipefail

SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
RG="dataq-rg"
ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
APP_API="2024-03-01"
JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"

# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
APPS_START=(dataq-harness-redis dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow-trigger)
APPS_STOP=(dataq-harness-airflow-trigger dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-redis)
TRIGGERS=(tr_customers_daily tr_orders_landed)
JOBS=(mockdata-orders mockdata-inventory mockdata-tracking mockdata-feedback mockdata-supply)

log() { printf '%s %s\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" "$*"; }

app_state() { az containerapp show -n "$1" -g "$RG" --query properties.runningStatus -o tsv 2>/dev/null; }

wait_app() { # wait_app <name> <wanted-state>
  local name=$1 want=$2 i
  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    [[ "$(app_state "$name")" == "$want" ]] && { log "  $name -> $want"; return 0; }
    sleep 5
  done
  log "  TIMEOUT: $name never reached $want"; return 1
}

cmd_status() {
  az containerapp list -g "$RG" --query "[?starts_with(name,'dataq-harness')].{name:name, running:properties.runningStatus}" -o table
  az datafactory trigger list -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --query "[].{name:name, state:properties.runtimeState}" -o table
  local j
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    printf '%s last execution: ' "$j"
    az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null |
      python3 -c "import json,sys; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['value']; print(v[0]['properties']['status']+' @ '+v[0]['properties']['startTime'] if v else 'none')"
  done
}

cmd_start() {
  local app t j
  log "Waking harness (billing ~CAD 0.70/hour while awake)..."
  for app in "${APPS_START[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/start?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Running
    if [[ "$app" == "dataq-harness-airflow" ]]; then
      log "  waiting for Airflow /health..."
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        curl -sf --max-time 10 "$AIRFLOW_URL/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { log "  Airflow healthy"; break; }
        sleep 10
      done
    fi
  done
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger start -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Started"
  done
  log "Harness is UP (mockdata crons stay suspended; 'run' fires them manually). Remember to '$0 stop' when done."
}

cmd_run() {
  local with_adf=${1:-} j execs=()
  log "Starting the 5 mockdata job executions (parallel)..."
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    local ename
    ename=$(az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/start?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name',''))")
    log "  $j -> execution ${ename:-<unknown>}"
    execs+=("$j:$ename")
  done
  log "Waiting for job executions to finish (timeout 35m each)..."
  local pair status
  for pair in "${execs[@]}"; do
    j=${pair%%:*}; local e=${pair#*:}
    [[ -z "$e" ]] && continue
    for _ in $(seq 1 70); do
      status=$(az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions/$e?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['properties']['status'])" 2>/dev/null || echo Unknown)
      [[ "$status" != "Running" && "$status" != "Unknown" ]] && break
      sleep 30
    done
    log "  $j: $status"
  done
  if [[ "$with_adf" == "--adf" ]]; then
    log "Triggering Flow-A ADF pipelines (needs live Snowflake!)..."
    local p rid
    for p in pl_flow_a_customers pl_flow_a_orders; do
      rid=$(az datafactory pipeline create-run -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$p" --query runId -o tsv)
      log "  $p -> run $rid"
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        status=$(az datafactory pipeline-run show -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --run-id "$rid" --query status -o tsv)
        [[ "$status" != "InProgress" && "$status" != "Queued" ]] && break
        sleep 15
      done
      log "  $p: $status"
    done
  fi
  log "Run phase done. Airflow DAGs fire on their own cron while awake (or trigger in the UI: $AIRFLOW_URL)."
}

cmd_stop() {
  local t j app
  log "Putting the harness back to sleep..."
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger stop -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Stopped"
  done
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && log "  job $j suspended" || true
  done
  for app in "${APPS_STOP[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/stop?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Stopped
  done
  log "Harness is DOWN (burn back to ~CAD 4.5/day, app only)."
}

case "${1:-}" in
  status) cmd_status ;;
  start)  cmd_start ;;
  run)    cmd_run "${2:-}" ;;
  stop)   cmd_stop ;;
  window) cmd_start; cmd_run "${2:-}"; cmd_stop ;;
  *) grep '^#' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//' | head -20; exit 1 ;;
esac

log "  $j -> execution ${ename:-<unknown>}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.
#
# The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
# awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
# it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.
#
# Usage:
#   harness_window.sh status           # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start            # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf]      # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
#                                      #   (+ --adf: manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*)
#   harness_window.sh stop             # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf]   # start -> run -> stop, one shot
#
# Job semantics: a suspended ACA job REFUSES manual starts, so `run` resumes each
# job, starts the execution, and suspends again at the end — everything executes
# on demand, never waiting for (or leaving armed) the cron schedules. `run` works
# standalone (mockdata jobs have no dependency on the Airflow apps).
#
# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
set -euo pipefail

SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
RG="dataq-rg"
ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
APP_API="2024-03-01"
JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"

# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
APPS_START=(dataq-harness-redis dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow-trigger)
APPS_STOP=(dataq-harness-airflow-trigger dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-redis)
TRIGGERS=(tr_customers_daily tr_orders_landed)
JOBS=(mockdata-orders mockdata-inventory mockdata-tracking mockdata-feedback mockdata-supply)

log() { printf '%s %s\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" "$*"; }

app_state() { az containerapp show -n "$1" -g "$RG" --query properties.runningStatus -o tsv 2>/dev/null; }

wait_app() { # wait_app <name> <wanted-state>
  local name=$1 want=$2 i
  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    [[ "$(app_state "$name")" == "$want" ]] && { log "  $name -> $want"; return 0; }
    sleep 5
  done
  log "  TIMEOUT: $name never reached $want"; return 1
}

cmd_status() {
  az containerapp list -g "$RG" --query "[?starts_with(name,'dataq-harness')].{name:name, running:properties.runningStatus}" -o table
  az datafactory trigger list -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --query "[].{name:name, state:properties.runtimeState}" -o table
  local j
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    printf '%s last execution: ' "$j"
    az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null |
      python3 -c "import json,sys; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['value']; print(v[0]['properties']['status']+' @ '+v[0]['properties']['startTime'] if v else 'none')"
  done
}

cmd_start() {
  local app t j
  log "Waking harness (billing ~CAD 0.70/hour while awake)..."
  for app in "${APPS_START[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/start?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Running
    if [[ "$app" == "dataq-harness-airflow" ]]; then
      log "  waiting for Airflow /health..."
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        curl -sf --max-time 10 "$AIRFLOW_URL/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { log "  Airflow healthy"; break; }
        sleep 10
      done
    fi
  done
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger start -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Started"
  done
  log "Harness is UP (mockdata crons stay suspended; 'run' fires them manually). Remember to '$0 stop' when done."
}

cmd_run() {
  local with_adf=${1:-} j execs=()
  log "Starting the 5 mockdata jobs as manual executions (parallel)..."
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    # a suspended job refuses manual starts -> resume first (idempotent), suspend again after the waits
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/resume?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    local ename
    ename=$(az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/start?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name',''))")
    log "  $j -> execution ${ename:-<unknown>}"
    execs+=("$j:$ename")
  done
  log "Waiting for job executions to finish (timeout 35m each)..."
  local pair status
  for pair in "${execs[@]}"; do
    j=${pair%%:*}; local e=${pair#*:}
    [[ -z "$e" ]] && continue
    for _ in $(seq 1 70); do
      status=$(az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions/$e?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['properties']['status'])" 2>/dev/null || echo Unknown)
      [[ "$status" != "Running" && "$status" != "Unknown" ]] && break
      sleep 30
    done
    log "  $j: $status"
  done
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
  done
  log "  mockdata jobs re-suspended (crons disarmed)"
  if [[ "$with_adf" == "--adf" ]]; then
    log "Triggering Flow-A ADF pipelines (needs live Snowflake!)..."
    local p rid
    for p in pl_flow_a_customers pl_flow_a_orders; do
      rid=$(az datafactory pipeline create-run -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$p" --query runId -o tsv)
      log "  $p -> run $rid"
      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
        status=$(az datafactory pipeline-run show -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --run-id "$rid" --query status -o tsv)
        [[ "$status" != "InProgress" && "$status" != "Queued" ]] && break
        sleep 15
      done
      log "  $p: $status"
    done
  fi
  log "Run phase done. Airflow DAGs fire on their own cron while awake (or trigger in the UI: $AIRFLOW_URL)."
}

cmd_stop() {
  local t j app
  log "Putting the harness back to sleep..."
  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
    az datafactory trigger stop -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Stopped"
  done
  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && log "  job $j suspended" || true
  done
  for app in "${APPS_STOP[@]}"; do
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/stop?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    wait_app "$app" Stopped
  done
  log "Harness is DOWN (burn back to ~CAD 4.5/day, app only)."
}

case "${1:-}" in
  status) cmd_status ;;
  start)  cmd_start ;;
  run)    cmd_run "${2:-}" ;;
  stop)   cmd_stop ;;
  window) cmd_start; cmd_run "${2:-}"; cmd_stop ;;
  *) grep '^#' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//' | head -20; exit 1 ;;
esac

lines: log "  $j: $status"
done
for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
-    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
+    # retry: suspend 409s while a start/resume operation is still provisioning
+    for _ in $(seq 1 8); do
+      az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && break
+      sleep 15
+    done
done
log "  mockdata jobs re-suspended (crons disarmed)"
if [[ "$with_adf" == "--adf" ]]; then

input.topic: stable REST API: trigger a DAG run (POST /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns) request body, basic auth backend, unpause DAG via PATCH is_paused

content.text:
### Pause Airflow DAG using cURL with Basic Authentication

Source: https://github.com/apache/airflow/blob/2.10.5/clients/python/README.md

This cURL command demonstrates how to pause an Apache Airflow DAG via the API using a PATCH request. It includes setting the 'Content-Type' header to 'application/json', providing basic authentication credentials, and sending a JSON payload to update the 'is_paused' status of the specified DAG.

```bash
curl -X PATCH 'https://example.com/api/v1/dags/{dag_id}?update_mask=is_paused' \
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--user "username:password" \
-d '{
    "is_paused": true
}'
```

--------------------------------

### Deprecated Airflow REST API Endpoints for DAGs, DAG Runs, and Tasks

Source: https://github.com/apache/airflow/blob/2.10.5/docs/apache-airflow/deprecated-rest-api-ref.rst

This section documents the deprecated REST API endpoints related to DAGs, DAG runs, and tasks within Apache Airflow. It includes methods for triggering DAGs, retrieving DAG run information, and accessing task details. Note that this API is disabled by default and requires 'enable_experimental_api' to be set to 'True' in the '[api]' section of the Airflow configuration to be active.

```APIDOC
POST /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs
  - Creates a dag_run for a given DAG ID.
  - Note: If execution_date is not specified, Airflow creates only one DAG run per second for a given DAG_ID. To create multiple DagRuns within one second, set 'replace_microseconds' to 'false' (boolean as string).
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - Request Body (JSON):
      - conf (optional): Configuration dictionary for the DAG run (e.g., {"key":"value"}).
      - execution_date (optional): The execution date for the DAG run in 'YYYY-mm-DDTHH:MM:SS.ssssss' format.
      - replace_microseconds (optional): Boolean as string ('true' or 'false') to allow multiple DAG runs within one second if 'false'.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs
  - Returns a list of Dag Runs for a specific DAG ID.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<string:dag_id>/dag_runs/<string:execution_date>
  - Returns a JSON with a dag_run's public instance variables.
  - Parameters:
    - dag_id: The ID of the DAG.
    - execution_date: The execution date of the DAG run in 'YYYY-mm-DDTHH:MM:SS' format (e.g., '2016-11-16T11:34:15').

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/tasks/<TASK_ID>
  - Returns information for a specific task within a DAG.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - TASK_ID: The ID of the task.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs/<string:execution_date>/tasks/<TASK_ID>
  - Returns a JSON with a task instance's public instance variables for a specific DAG run.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - execution_date: The execution date of the DAG run in 'YYYY-mm-DDTHH:MM:SS' format (e.g., '2016-11-16T11:34:15').
    - TASK_ID: The ID of the task.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/paused/<string:paused>
  - Sets the paused state of a DAG.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - paused: Must be 'true' to pause a DAG and 'false' to unpause.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/paused
  - Returns the paused state of a DAG.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.

GET /api/experimental/latest_runs
  - Returns the latest DagRun for each DAG, formatted for the UI.

GET /api/experimental/lineage/<DAG_ID>/<string:execution_date>/
  - Returns the lineage information for a specific DAG run.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - execution_date: The execution date of the DAG run.
```

```bash
curl -X POST \
    'http://localhost:8080/api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs' \
    --header 'Cache-Control: no-cache' \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --data '{"conf":"{\"key\":\"value\"}"}'
```

```bash
curl -X POST  \
    'http://localhost:8080/api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs' \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --header 'Cache-Control: no-cache' \
    --data '{"replace_microseconds":"false"}'
```

--------------------------------

### Airflow Stable API Permissions: DAG and DAG Run Management Endpoints

toolUseResult.text:
### Pause Airflow DAG using cURL with Basic Authentication

Source: https://github.com/apache/airflow/blob/2.10.5/clients/python/README.md

This cURL command demonstrates how to pause an Apache Airflow DAG via the API using a PATCH request. It includes setting the 'Content-Type' header to 'application/json', providing basic authentication credentials, and sending a JSON payload to update the 'is_paused' status of the specified DAG.

```bash
curl -X PATCH 'https://example.com/api/v1/dags/{dag_id}?update_mask=is_paused' \
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--user "username:password" \
-d '{
    "is_paused": true
}'
```

--------------------------------

### Deprecated Airflow REST API Endpoints for DAGs, DAG Runs, and Tasks

Source: https://github.com/apache/airflow/blob/2.10.5/docs/apache-airflow/deprecated-rest-api-ref.rst

This section documents the deprecated REST API endpoints related to DAGs, DAG runs, and tasks within Apache Airflow. It includes methods for triggering DAGs, retrieving DAG run information, and accessing task details. Note that this API is disabled by default and requires 'enable_experimental_api' to be set to 'True' in the '[api]' section of the Airflow configuration to be active.

```APIDOC
POST /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs
  - Creates a dag_run for a given DAG ID.
  - Note: If execution_date is not specified, Airflow creates only one DAG run per second for a given DAG_ID. To create multiple DagRuns within one second, set 'replace_microseconds' to 'false' (boolean as string).
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - Request Body (JSON):
      - conf (optional): Configuration dictionary for the DAG run (e.g., {"key":"value"}).
      - execution_date (optional): The execution date for the DAG run in 'YYYY-mm-DDTHH:MM:SS.ssssss' format.
      - replace_microseconds (optional): Boolean as string ('true' or 'false') to allow multiple DAG runs within one second if 'false'.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs
  - Returns a list of Dag Runs for a specific DAG ID.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<string:dag_id>/dag_runs/<string:execution_date>
  - Returns a JSON with a dag_run's public instance variables.
  - Parameters:
    - dag_id: The ID of the DAG.
    - execution_date: The execution date of the DAG run in 'YYYY-mm-DDTHH:MM:SS' format (e.g., '2016-11-16T11:34:15').

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/tasks/<TASK_ID>
  - Returns information for a specific task within a DAG.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - TASK_ID: The ID of the task.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs/<string:execution_date>/tasks/<TASK_ID>
  - Returns a JSON with a task instance's public instance variables for a specific DAG run.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - execution_date: The execution date of the DAG run in 'YYYY-mm-DDTHH:MM:SS' format (e.g., '2016-11-16T11:34:15').
    - TASK_ID: The ID of the task.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/paused/<string:paused>
  - Sets the paused state of a DAG.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - paused: Must be 'true' to pause a DAG and 'false' to unpause.

GET /api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/paused
  - Returns the paused state of a DAG.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.

GET /api/experimental/latest_runs
  - Returns the latest DagRun for each DAG, formatted for the UI.

GET /api/experimental/lineage/<DAG_ID>/<string:execution_date>/
  - Returns the lineage information for a specific DAG run.
  - Parameters:
    - DAG_ID: The ID of the DAG.
    - execution_date: The execution date of the DAG run.
```

```bash
curl -X POST \
    'http://localhost:8080/api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs' \
    --header 'Cache-Control: no-cache' \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --data '{"conf":"{\"key\":\"value\"}"}'
```

```bash
curl -X POST  \
    'http://localhost:8080/api/experimental/dags/<DAG_ID>/dag_runs' \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --header 'Cache-Control: no-cache' \
    --data '{"replace_microseconds":"false"}'
```

#   harness_window.sh status           # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start            # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf]      # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
#                                      #   (+ --adf: manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*)
#   harness_window.sh stop             # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf]   # start -> run -> stop, one shot
input.new_string:
#   harness_window.sh status                  # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start                    # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf] [--dags]     # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
#                                              #   --adf:  manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*
#                                              #   --dags: manual-trigger the 3 cron DAGs via the Airflow REST API
#                                              #     (POST /api/v1/dags/{id}/dagRuns, basic auth; unpauses first --
#                                              #      a run created on a paused DAG never executes). Needs the
#                                              #      harness awake. flow_a_payments_event is NOT force-triggered:
#                                              #      it requires an Event Grid conf payload and fires naturally
